danger_signals.visual_form: Animated enemy sprites with distinct movement patterns
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring evasion or bomb placement

danger_signals.visual_form: Animated fuse on placed bombs, sprite animation acceleration
Cross-pattern fire sprite propagation across tiles
danger_signals.meaning: Active lethal area; player must be outside blast range

danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct sprite animations preceding attack behavior

safety_signals.visual_form: Empty floor tiles with no enemies, bombs, or fire sprites
safety_signals.meaning: Safe position for movement and regrouping
Traversable area with reduced bomb placement strategy required

safety_signals.visual_form: Distinct colored item sprites (varies by power type)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Distinct character sprite design (standard Bomberman silhouette)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player 1 starting zone position in arena

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Distinct character sprite design (standard Bomberman silhouette with color swap)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player 2 starting zone position, typically opposite corner from P1

faction_indicators.color_identity: Varies by enemy type; distinct from playable characters
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Unique sprite designs per enemy archetype
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Different animation states reflecting aggression level or special abilities

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Soft blocks arranged in maze pattern; hard blocks static; players positioned at starting corners; no active bombs or flames
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies dormant or in patrol state; no projectile activity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bomb sprites placed on grid; fuse animation active; destroyed blocks create visual gaps; power-ups revealed as blocks clear
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies pursue player; collision detection active; bomb detonation sequences trigger

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Narrow safe zones remaining; dense fire sprite patterns; limited clear tiles
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies become more aggressive; movement becomes restricted

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Power-up sprite disappears; player sprite may show subtle visual change or equipment indicator
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased bomb blast range or placement speed; enhanced movement capabilities

environmental_storytelling.example: Soft block wall patterns create natural choke points and strategic bomb placement zones; hard block placement defines arena flow
Placement of range/speed upgrades in accessible vs. contested zones signals strategic difficulty and player progression pacing
Player 1 and Player 2 spawned in opposite corners with similar terrain sight-lines suggests balanced competitive design intent

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Sprite animates in cardinal direction; responds immediately to input
Brief placement animation; bomb sprite appears on tile; player forced to occupy adjacent tile
Slow movement loops or stationary animation; predictable path patterns
Animation acceleration; directed movement toward player; projectile/charge animations triggered
Cross-pattern expansion; collision damage on contact; automatic despawn after brief duration

notes: Super Bomberman 5 uses classic grid-based arena design with visual clarity between safe/dangerous zones. Faction distinction relies on character color palette swap (white vs. black) rather than complex faction systems. World state communication is primarily mechanical (bomb placement, fire expansion) rather than narrative-driven. Power-up discovery serves as both mechanical progression and visual reward for strategic block destruction. The game prioritizes immediate visual feedback for collision and blast interaction.
